description: Serve /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server (RFC 8414) advertising issuer, authorization_endpoint, token_endpoint, jwks_uri (no registration_endpoint — CIMD), response_types, grant_types, code_challenge_methods=[S256], scopes_supported.
PRM (/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource) advertises authorization_servers: [https://<base>] (Alga itself) instead of the raw IdP issuers.
PRM advertises a non-empty scopes_supported consistent with the AS metadata.
Serve /.well-known/jwks.json publishing Alga's MCP token signing public key(s).
Signing key material managed via the secret provider; generated/loaded at startup; never logged.
Signing key rotation supported (kid in JWT header; JWKS can publish current + previous).
/oauth/authorize accepts authorization_code params and validates client_id and exact redirect_uri.
PKCE required: reject authorize requests missing code_challenge or using a method other than S256.
Preserve and return the client state parameter unchanged on redirect.
Accept the resource indicator (RFC 8707) and bind the requested MCP resource into the issued token's audience.
If the user already has an Alga session, proceed within it (no second login).
If no Alga session, redirect to normal Alga login and return to /authorize afterward (round-trip preserved).
Render a consent screen (client name, tenant, access description, Approve/Deny).
Persist consent trust-on-first-use per (user, client); subsequent connects honor the policy.
Issue a short-lived, signed authorization code bound to (user, tenant, client_id, redirect_uri, PKCE challenge, resource).
Redirect back to the client's redirect_uri with code + state on approval; return an OAuth error on deny.
/oauth/token authorization_code grant: verify code, PKCE code_verifier, and redirect_uri match.
Issue an Alga-signed JWT access token (iss=<base>, aud=<base>/api/mcp, sub=user, tenant, scope, client_id, short TTL).
Issue a rotating refresh token alongside the access token.
refresh_token grant: validate + rotate refresh token, issue a fresh access token.
Reject reused/expired authorization codes and reused (replayed) refresh tokens.
Return RFC 6749-compliant token error responses (invalid_grant, invalid_client, etc.).
CIMD: accept an https URL as client_id, fetch the client metadata document, and validate the requested redirect_uri against it.
CIMD fetch is SSRF-safe: https-only, host validation, timeout, response size cap, cached.
Persist client records derived from CIMD (client_id URL, fetched redirect_uris, client name, cached metadata, fetched_at).
Persist grants: authorization codes, refresh tokens, consent, and revocation state (keyed by tenant/user/client).

prdRefs: Token validation at the MCP RS
description: jsonRpcServer accepts Alga-issued access tokens: verify iss, aud=<base>/api/mcp, signature (JWKS/local key), and exp.

prdRefs: Token validation at the MCP RS
description: On RS validation, check the grant is not revoked before accepting the token.

prdRefs: Token validation at the MCP RS
description: Resolve user_id + tenant from the Alga token and dispatch tool calls under the user's identity (mint a user-scoped short-lived dispatch key).
Tool calls run under the user's existing RBAC/ABAC; a permission the user lacks is denied.
RS still accepts legacy external-IdP agent tokens (existing authenticateAgentToken path) unchanged — backward compatible.
/oauth/revoke (RFC 7009) revokes a grant (access + refresh).
Settings → MCP: list a user's connected MCP clients (v1).
Settings → MCP: disconnect/revoke a connected client from the UI (v1).
All new AS + revoke endpoints are EE-gated (404/disabled in CE).
Expose new AS capability through the @product/mcp seam (ee implementation + oss stub) consistent with existing pattern.
DB migrations create mcp_oauth_clients and mcp_oauth_grants tables with tenant scoping and required indexes.
Feature-flag / dark-release gating (reuse mcp-server flag) so the AS can ship off-by-default.
